1. What is the correct starting point of every C++ program?

    A. start()
    B. main()
    C. run()
    D. begin()

2. Is C++ case-sensitive?
    Explain what happens if you write:

    int Number;
    int number;

3. What is the difference between:

    int x; and int x = 5;

4. What will this print?
    int x = 5;
    cout << sizeof(x);

5. Which of the following is a valid identifier?

    A. 2number
    B. total-value
    C. _count
    D. float

6. What is the output?

    int a = 3;
    int b = 4;
    cout << a + b;

7. What is the difference between:

    const int x = 10; and int x = 10;

8. What is wrong with this code?
    int main()
    {
        int x;
        cout << x;
    }

9. What is the size (usually) of:

    char
    int
    double
    (You may answer in bytes.)

10. Explain in your own words:
    What is Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)?
